LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RAJORSHI MITRA'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND STAYING OR DIMISSING ACTION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION COMPELLING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. ACTION IS STAYED. IN ADDITION, PARTIES TO EXCHANGE LISTS OF AT LEAST FOUR ARBITRATORS WITHIN 21 DAYS AND THEREAFTER SELECTION OF ARBITRATOR TO BE MADE WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM TODAY (3/24/09). GEOGRAPHIC BOUNDARIES FOR ARBITRATOR MAY GO AS FAR AS PALO ALTO. PARTIES AGREE THAT PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL'S OFFICE MAY ALSO BE USED AS A LOCATION FOR THE ARBITRATION PROCESS. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Or Dimissing Action
Off Calendar Mar-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RAJORSHI MITRA'S Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Or Dimissing Action.
APPEARANCES: Fredrik Emilson, Esq. of Cornerstone Law Group 357-2099 for pltfs & opposing parties; Daniel A. Street, Esq. of The Law Office of Daniel A. Street 916/920-9005 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall).
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion compelling arbitration is granted. Action is Stayed. In addition, parties to exchange lists of at least four arbitrators within 21 days and thereafter selection of arbitrator to be made within 30 days from today (3/24/09). Geographic boundaries for arbitrator may go as far as Palo Alto. Parties agree that plaintiffs' counsel's office may also be used as a location for the arbitration process. Defendant to prepare proposed form of order. (302/CWW/XAM) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
